expectations.evidence: Session report (lines 19-35) explicitly documents session start: 'Started session by executing Trip Orchestrator skill's session-start protocol: reviewed CLAUDE.md, TASKS.md, and last session log (Mar 28A).' Lists project status (Phase 5 complete, Phase 6 active), travelers, budget, and current phase context before proceeding.

Context at Session Start section (lines 19-35) provides: Phase status (Phase 5 Activities locked, Phase 6 Restaurants active), active tasks (restaurant research for 3 cities), blockers (ryokan waiting since Mar 25), and TASKS.md items listed. Concisely summarizes current state before work begins.

Session report (lines 108-118) documents creation of memory/research/restaurant-research-kyoto.md (1,200+ words) as primary deliverable. This is explicitly a research file in memory/research/, not a guide. Sections include: scope, comparison table (18 restaurants), ruled-out rationale, day-by-day recommendations.

Session report (lines 159-171) includes 'Verification Checklist (Next Session)' with explicit statement: 'Before creating guide file and sending reservation emails: ... Once verified, proceed to: 1. Create Kyoto restaurant guide file (post-user-approval recommendations)'. Guide creation explicitly deferred pending user approval.

Day-by-day meal slot strategy table (session report lines 94-101) presents recommendations slotted into Oct 18-21 Kyoto schedule: arrival dinner, Oct 19 dinner, lunch/dinner combos, Oct 21 dinner. Meals are integrated into the activity schedule context, not presented as category list. kyoto-restaurant-recommendations.md includes 'Proposed 4-Night Schedule' (lines 98-106).

Methodology section (lines 41-72) documents Phase 3 'Cross-Validation': 'Checked ratings across 2–3 sources for consistency. Verified vegetarian accommodation specifics for each option. Reviewed recent reviews (past 6–12 months).' research-sources.md file (referenced in outputs) documents 20+ sources consulted including Michelin Guide, Tabelog, TripAdvisor, Google Maps, multiple vegan/vegetarian travel guides.
